title: Lycopodioides tenuissima
taxonomy: Lycopodioides tenuissima belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida and the division Lycopodiophyta. It is further classified as a genus and species within the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant is a type of lycopod, which is an ancient group of vascular plants that have been around for millions of years. The classification of Lycopodioides tenuissima reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Its classification is a key area of study for botanists and plant scientists.

intro: Lycopodioides tenuissima, also known as club moss, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Lycopodiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive club-shaped sporophylls. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as respiratory issues and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Lycopodioides tenuissima include lignans, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
